MINI MINUTES FOR JUN-03-2015 09:30 AM FOR DEPT 302

LAW AND MOTION, 302, DEFENDANT EQUILON ENTERPRISES LLC, DBA SHELL OIL PRODUCTS US'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON PLAINTIFF'S COMPLAINT IS CONTINUED TO JUNE 15, 2015 AT 9:30 A.M. IN DEPT. 302 TO GIVE DEFENDANT THE OPPORTUNITY TO COMPLY WITH LOCAL RULE 2.6B. DEFENDANT MUST DELIVER COURTESY COPIES OF THE MOVING PAPERS TO DEPARTMENT 302, WITH A COVER LETTER STATING THE NEW HEARING DATE, NO LATER THAN JUNE 3, 2015 AT 4:00 PM. ANY EXHIBIT MUST BE SEPARATED BY AN EXHIBIT TAB PER CRC 3.1110(F). JUDGE HAROLD KAHN; CLERK FELICIA GREEN (302/HK)
MEMORANDUM OF POINTS AND AUTHORITIES IN SUPPORT OF OBJECTION TO JUDGE KHAN, 170.6 (TRANSACTION ID # 57403220) FILED BY DEFENDANT EQUILON ENTERPRISES LLC, DBA SHELL OIL PRODUCTS US
DECLARATION OF NICOLE L. JONES ISO DEFENDANTS OBJECTION TO HON. HAROLD KAHN (TRANSACTION ID # 57403220) FILED BY DEFENDANT EQUILON ENTERPRISES LLC, DBA SHELL OIL PRODUCTS US

LAW AND MOTION, 302, A CCP 170.1 CHALLENGE WAS EXERCISED IN OPEN COURT BY DEFENDANT EQUILON ENTERPRISES DBA SHELL OIL PRODUCT US BECAUSE OF A DISCLOSURE BY JUDGE KAHN THAT HIS BROTHER'S WIFE BROTHER IS REPRESENTED BY THE LAW FIRM REPRESENTING PLAINTIFF. DEFENDANT EQUILON ENTERPRISES LLC, DBA SHELL OIL PRODUCTS US' MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT ON PLAINTIFFS COMPLAINT IS PENDING AWAITING FOR JUDGE KAHN'S ANSWER TO THE CCP 170.1 CHALLENGE AGAINST HIM. Off Calendar Sep-23-2015 Continued To May-09-16 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 206

Defendant Equilon Enterprises Dba Shell Oil Product Us Motion For Summary Judgment
Off Calendar Aug-17-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

APPEARANCES - ATTY. CRAIG PETERS OF THE VEEN FIRM, TEL. NO. 1-415-673-4800 APPEARING FOR THE PLAINTIFF;
ATTY. CHRISTOPHER J. NEVIS & ATTY. NICOLE L. JONES APPEARING FOR THE DEFENDANT.

RULING - A R G U E D; The Court adopted its tentative ruling. Defendant Equilon Enterprises dba Shell Oil Product US' Motion For Summary Judgment is DENIED. There is a genuine issue of material fact as to whether defendant exercised control over the refinery in a manner that affirmatively contributed to plaintiff's injuries. There is also a genuine issue of material fact as to whether Defendant Shell Oil's actual exercise of control over its refinery affected the manner in which Contra Costa Electric conducted its work such that Plaintiff was not "entirely free to do the work in his own way." (Rest.2d Torts, 414, com. c, p. 388.) "[A] hirer of an independent contractor is not liable to an employee of the contractor merely because the hirer retained control over safety conditions at a worksite, but that a hirer is liable to an employee of a contractor insofar as a hirer's exercise of retained control affirmatively contributed to the employee's injuries." (Hooker v. Dep't of Transp. (2002) 27 Cal.4th 198, 202.) The court will rule on the Evidentiary objections. Order signed in open court. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Reporter : Melanie Gheno, CSR # 7489 =302/eg
